summary: markable-table typosquat executes remote code on install via base64-obfuscated eval of fetched payload
details: markable-table is a typosquat of markdown-table. package.json declares scripts.preinstall = 'node index.js', causing index.js to run on npm install. index.js contains a base64-encoded string that is decoded with Buffer.from(b4,'base64').toString('utf-8'), and the identifier 'eval' is reconstructed from the char-code array [101,118,97,108] via String.fromCharCode and invoked through globalThis[tag] to hide the call from static scanners. The decoded payload fetches JavaScript from https://everydaynodechecker-39143n.vercel.app/api/key?mem=root0 and eval's the response, yielding arbitrary remote code execution on the installer machine under the user running npm install. The same code path also fires on require() of the package's main entry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
